LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DREXEL BRADSHAW, BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED: PLAINTIFF'S LATE OPPOSITION CONSIDERED. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THIS WILL BE THE LAST OPPORTUNITY FOR THE PLAINTIFF TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF MUST CAPTION EACH CAUSE OF ACTION IDENTIFYING WHICH DEFENDANT IT REFERS TO. (CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 2.112.) AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LEGAL MALPRACTICE, PLAINTIFF MUST CLEARLY ALLEGE WHAT DUTY DEFENDANTS OWED TO PLAINTIFF, HOW DEFENDANTS BREACHED THE DUTY, AND THAT PLAINTIFF WOULD HAVE PREVAILED IN THE UNDERLYING LAW SUIT. AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE WITH SPECIFICITY THAT DEFENDANT KNEW HIS REPRESENTATIONS TO REMOVE MINOR'S COUNSEL WERE FALSE AND ALLEGE DAMAGES WITH PARTICULARITY. AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE MISREPRESENTATION, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE WHY DEFENDANT HAD NO REASONABLE GROUNDS TO BELIEVE HIS REPRESENTATIONS WERE TRUE AND ALLEGE SPECIFIC DAMAGES. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST STATE WHETHER THE CONTRACT WAS ORAL, WRITTEN OR IMPLIED BY CONDUCT, THE MATERIAL TERMS OF THE CONTRACT, HOW DEFENDANTS BREACHED THE CONTRACT, AND ALLEGE THAT SHE FULFILLED HER OBLIGATIONS AND COMPLIED WITH THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE CONTRACT. PLAINTIFF MUST DESCRIBE A MEASURABLE INJURY OR DAMAGES RESULTING FROM THE BREACH. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DREXEL BRADSHAW AND BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, P.C. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT (TRANSFERRED FROM DEPT. 301, PER CHALLENGE TO JUDGE BUSCH). PRO PER PLAINTIFF FAILED TO GIVE NOTICE, PURSUANT TO SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 8.3. THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH FORTY-FIVE DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE CAUSATION AND PLEAD AROUND THE UNCLEAN HANDS DEFENSE. AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PARTICULARLY ALLEGE DEFENDANT'S KNOWLEDGE OF FALSITY AND DAMAGES. AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST SPECIFY WHY DEFENDANTS HAD NO REASONABLE GROUNDS TO BELIEVE HIS REPRESENTATIONS WERE TRUE AND DAMAGES. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS REGARDING THE BREACH OF CONTRACT TERMS AND DAMAGES, AND PLEAD AROUND THE DEFENSE OF PLAINTIFF'S NON-PERFORMANCE. PARTIES WAIVE NOTICE OF TODAY'S RULING. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBBINE, CSR #5797

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - overruled, 10 days leave to answer. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. 10 days to answer from notice of entry of order. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-19-2010 Continued To Sep-17-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DREXEL BRADSHAW, BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Appearances:
Deeanna Brossoit (925.989.3179) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona via Court Call
Drexel A. Bradshaw of Bradshaw & Associates (433.4800) for defendants and moving parties
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: Plaintiff's late opposition considered. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. This will be the last opportunity for the plaintiff to amend the complaint. Plaintiff must caption each cause of action identifying which defendant it refers to. (California Rule of Court 2.112.) As to the 1st cause of action for legal malpractice, plaintiff must clearly allege what duty defendants owed to plaintiff, how defendants breached the duty, and that plaintiff would have prevailed in the underlying law suit. As to the 2nd cause of action for fraud, plaintiff must allege with specificity that defendant knew his representations to remove minor's counsel were false and allege damages with particularity. As to the 3rd cause of action for negligence misrepresentation, plaintiff must allege why defendant had no reasonable grounds to believe his representations were true and allege specific damages. As to the 4th cause of action, plaintiff must state whether the contract was oral, written or implied by conduct, the material terms of the contract, how defendants breached the contract, and allege that she fulfilled her obligations and complied with the terms and conditions of the contract. Plaintiff must describe a measurable injury or damages resulting from the breach. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Defendants Drexel Bradshaw And Bradshaw & Associates, P.C. Demurrer To Complaint (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Challenge To Judge Busch)
Off Calendar Feb-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendants Drexel Bradshaw and Bradshaw & Associates, P.C. Demurrer To Complaint (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Challenge To Judge Busch). Appearances: Deeanna M. Brossoit, Plaintiff in Pro Per appearing and opposing party; Meredithe Kreis, Esq. of Bradshaw & Associates (415) 433-4800, appearing for Defendants and Moving party. Pro Per Plaintiff failed to give notice, pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 8.3. The Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with forty-five days leave to amend. As to 1st cause of action, plaintiff must allege causation and plead around the unclean hands defense. As to the 2nd cause of action, plaintiff must particularly allege defendant's knowledge of falsity and damages. As to the 3rd cause of action, plaintiff must specify why defendants had no reasonable grounds to believe his representations were true and damages. As to the 4th cause of action, plaintiff must allege facts regarding the breach of contract terms and damages, and plead around the defense of plaintiff's non-performance. Parties waive notice of today's ruling. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbbine, CSR #5797
